data.title: Wall Street Week Ahead
data.description:
Listen on the go! A daily podcast of Wall Street Breakfast will be available by 8:00 a.m. on Seeking Alpha, iTunes, Spotify.

Investors this week will be on the lookout for the print of the PCE Price Index for January on Friday. That's the Fed's preferred inflation gauge. The second estimate for fourth-quarter gross domestic product in the U.S. is due Thursday, along with durable goods data for last month.

The highlight of next week’s earnings calendar will be the world’s most valuable chipmaker, Nvidia (NVDA) on Wednesday. Analysts at KeyBanc Capital Markets expect the company to offer up “strong” results and guidance when it reports quarterly results next week, despite the GB200 NVL constraints it has faced.

Other notable earnings names include Home Depot (HD), Intuit (INTU), Salesforce (CRM), and Workday (WDAY).

Earnings spotlight: Monday, February 24 - ONEOK (OKE), Public Storage (PSA), Trip.com Group (TCOM), Zoom Communications (ZM), and SBA Communications (SBAC). See the full earnings calendar.
Earnings spotlight: Tuesday, February 25 - Home Depot, Intuit, Workday, Sempra (SRE), and Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP). See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Wednesday, February 26 - Nvidia, Salesforce, Lowe’s (LOW), TJX (TJX), and Stellantis (STLA). See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Thursday, February 27 - Vistra (VST), HP (HPQ), Rocket (RKT), Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), and NetApp (NTAP). See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Friday, February 28 - EOG Resources (EOG), Chart Industries (GTLS), and AES (AES). See the full earnings calendar.

Investing Group Spotlight

Nvidia (NVDA) is set to report earnings this week on Feb. 26 after the bell. The market expects about $38.1B in sales and $0.84 in normalized EPS. The top-line number will be especially important here, as the market will look for signs of continued growth, specifically in the lucrative enterprise AI GPU segment.

While Nvidia guided to only $37.5B in sales for Q4, Nvidia often sandbags its forecasts, making it easier to provide better-than-anticipated results for the AI market leader. Therefore, Nvidia could provide $38-39B in sales while potentially delivering a minor beat on the EPS side. Guidance is particularly crucial, as the market wants to see continued robust demand for Nvidia's AI products despite the recent DeepSeek and other concerning events.

Despite the high expectations, I anticipate Nvidia can provide another constructive earnings report, which should support its stock price trajectory and the trajectory of other high-quality AI picks. Discover more analysis from Victor Dergunov with his SA Investing Group service - The Financial Prophet.

FSMD strategy

Fidelity Small-Mid Multifactor ETF (NYSEARCA:FSMD) started investing operations on 02/26/2019 and tracks the Fidelity Small-Mid Multifactor Index. FSMD has 595 holdings, a 30-day SEC yield of 1.22% and an expense ratio of 0.16%. As described in the prospectus by Fidelity, the underlying index…

…is designed to reflect the performance of stocks of small- and mid-capitalization U.S. companies with attractive valuations, high quality profiles, positive momentum signals, and lower volatility than the broader market.

In fact, five factors are involved in the strategy: value, quality, momentum, volatility, and also size (eligible stocks are in a small and mid-cap universe). The fund's turnover rate in the most recent fiscal year was 50%. This article will use as a benchmark the S&P SmallCap 600 Index, represented by iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF (IJR).

FSMD portfolio

The portfolio is almost exclusively invested in U.S. companies (about 99% of asset value), with a focus on industrials (20%) and financials (18.2%). FSMD also has significant exposure in consumer discretionary, healthcare and technology (each of them between 11% and 13%). Other sectors are below 7%. FSMD slightly overweights industrials, but the sector breakdown is not much different from the benchmark.

The portfolio is highly diversified and risks related to individual companies are very low. Indeed, the top 10 holdings, listed in the next table, represent only 4.5% of asset value, and the heaviest position weighs 0.54%.

data.title: My Top 10 REITs For 2025 (Revisited)
data.description:
In case you missed it, back in late December 2024, I published my “Top 10 REITs for 2025”.

As I mentioned in the article, my objective with the article was to “screen for the widest moat REITs that are also cheap”.

By “moat” I mean that I wanted to provide readers with a list of high-quality REITs that offer unique competitive advantages.

In addition, my goal was (and still is) to utilize fundamental analysis to build a basket of real estate stocks that deliver meaningful upside in 2025.

That goal (or target) was to deliver 29% annualized returns.

Thus far, roughly 52 days into the new year, the portfolio has returned an average of 5.4%.

This translates into a run-rate (annualizing 5.4%) of 38%...

This means that the portfolio is on target to meet the objective.

However, it’s always important to pay close attention to your stocks to determine if there are any changes in fundamentals or market conditions.

Which is the reason that I decided to revisit the “top 10” list.

Thus far the Vanguard Real Estate ETF (VNQ) has returned 2.9% which means that my basket of 10 REITs has doubled the results of VNQ.

So, without further ado, let’s get started…

EastGroup: +12.7% Since January 1st

EastGroup Properties (EGP) is an industrial REIT focused on properties in major Sunbelt markets throughout the U.S. with an emphasis in the states of Texas (34%), Florida (25%), California (17%), Arizona (8%) and North Carolina (5%).

The REIT is differentiated by its focus on “flex space” space, primarily customers leasing out 20,000 to 100,000 square feet in multi-tenant properties located in supply-constrained markets. The sites are strategically located with a focus on “last mile” e-commerce.

The portfolio is highly diversified with the top 10 customer customers accounting for only 7.2% of revenue and 75% of overall revenue is generated from tenants that lease under 100,000 square feet of space. I’m happy to see EastGroup in my hometown of Greenville, SC.

EastGroup also has a strong balance sheet, with debt to market cap of just 15% and a debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 3.4x. The company’s fixed charge coverage ratio is 11.5x.

The company has maintained strict capital markets discipline, as evidenced by the fact that it has increased or maintained its dividend for 32 consecutive years and has increased the dividend in 29 of the past 32 years (including each of the last 13 years).

The payout ratio is just around 64% giving the company ample cushion to keep growing the dividend.

In Q4-25, EastGroup’s FFO per share was $2.15 compared to $2.03 for the same quarter last year, an increase of 5.9%. It announced 2025 FFO guidance in the range of $2.05 to $2.13 per share (Q1) and $8.80 to $9.00 for the year. Those midpoints represent increases of 5.6% and 7.1%.

EGP has returned 12.7% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return another 20% by YE 2025, which equates to around 32% annualized returns in 2025. Thus, I maintain a BUY rating as shares are now trading at 27.0x (normal is 31.2x) with a dividend yield of 3.2%.

Terreno: +13.5% Since January 1st

Terreno Realty (TRNO) is an industrial REIT that owns 299 buildings (19.3 million square feet) leaded to 670 customers. The properties are warehouse/distribution (79.7%), flex (3.4%), transshipment aka truck terminals(6.0%) or improved land parcels (10.9%), depending on the submarket.

Terreno owns infill industrial real estate in six major coastal U.S. markets. Six markets that consistently demonstrate the strong operating fundamentals of lower vacancy and higher rent growth: New York City/Northern New Jersey, Los Angeles, Miami, San Francisco Bay Area, Seattle, and Washington, D.C.

Terreno seeks superior market fundamentals with strong demand generators (high population densities, high volume distribution points, logistics infrastructure).

Terreno has a strong balance sheet with a conservative capital structure. The company maintains a fixed charge coverage ratio in excess of 2.0x and net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA ratio of below 5.0x Fitch has assigned an issuer rating of BBB+ with a stable outlook.

As of December 31, 2024, the balance outstanding on Terreno’s $600 million revolving credit facility was approximately $82 million and the company has no debt maturing in 2025.

Terreno has sector-leading Cash SSNOI growth of 11.1% (since IPO) and dividend growth is 12.3% CAGR (since 2011 initiation).

TRNO has returned 13.5% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return another 30% by YE 2025, which equates to around 40% annualized returns in 2025. Thus, I maintain a STRONG BUY rating as shares are now trading at 35.0x (normal is 45x) with a dividend yield of 2.9%.

Rexford: +5.7% since January 1st

Rexford (REXR) is an industrial REIT that owns 425 properties (51 million square feet) located in primarily Southern California. The company has focused on this market because of the high demand and low supply in which the company’s primary “moat” is its “boots on the ground” model in which it serves over 1,600 customers.

In other words, Rexford has an “informational advantage” through deep domain expertise & decades of proprietary research. In addition, diminishing new supply in Southern CA supports future rent growth as the company has relative pricing power of its REIT peers.

Rexford also maintains a strong balance sheet with low leverage: net debt-to-EBITDA of 4.6x and $1.4 billion of liquidity, including nearly full availability on the $1 billion revolver. Net Debt to Enterprise Value is just 26.5% and strong ratings from all rating agencies (BBB+, BBB+, Baa2).

For 2024, Rexford delivered 7% growth in core FFO per share and the company established 2025 core FFO guidance of $2.37 to $2.41 per share.

REXR has returned 5.7% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return another 30% by YE 2025, which equates to around 35% annualized returns in 2025. Thus, I maintain a STRONG BUY rating as shares are now trading at 20.5x (normal is 40x) with a dividend yield of 4.3%.

VICI Properties: +7.1% since January 1st

VICI Properties (VICI) is a net lease REIT that owns 93 experiential properties across a geographically diverse portfolio consisting of 54 gaming properties and 39 other experiential properties across the U.S. and Canada.

The portfolio is comprised of approximately 127 million square feet and features approximately 60,300 hotel rooms and over 500 restaurants, bars, nightclubs and sportsbooks.

In addition to equity investments, VICI has also expanded into loans and construction financing for firms like Great Wolf Lodge, Canyon Ranch, Cabot, Kalahari and more recently Cain International and Eldridge Industries.

This latest deal (Cain and Eldridge) is a proposed $300 million investment into a mezzanine loan related to the development of One Beverly Hills, a 17.5 acre luxury mixed-use project at the intersection of Santa Monica Boulevard and Wilshire Boulevard – the heart of Beverly Hills, California.

As for me, I like VICI’s expanded business model as the company seeks out yield-enhancing loans to drive continuous innovation and improvement within the experiential operating model. Most of these deals have a pathway to real estate ownership, ensuring strong sustained and sustainable rent coverage.

VICI has maintained strict capital markets discipline, with net debt to annualized Q4 adjusted EBITDA of 5.3x and a weighted average interest rate of 4.41%. On November 18, 2024, Moody’s upgraded VICI giving the net lease REIT investment-grade credit rating across all three agencies. (in the summer of 2017 VICI had total leverage of roughly 10.5x debt-to-EBITDA).

In Q4-24, VICI’s AFFO per share was $0.57, an increase of 3.6%, compared to $0.55 for Q4-23. For the full year 2024, AFFO per share was $2.26, an increase of 5.1%, compared to $2.15 for the full year 2023. The company guided AFFO per share of $2.32 to $2.35 for 2025. 3.3% at the midpoint.

VICI has returned 7.1% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return another 20% by YE 2025, which equates to around 27% annualized returns in 2025. Thus, I maintain a BUY rating as shares are now trading at 13.8x (normal is 16x) with a dividend yield of 5.5%.

American Tower: +4.4% Since January 1st

American Tower (AMT) is an infrastructure REIT with operations in the major markets of the Americas, Europe, Africa, Asia and Oceania. The company owns 148,000 tower sites and 28 data centers, mainly located in US metro areas.

American Tower has solid growth metrics with a solid dividend. From 2014 to 2024, the company delivered (1) 10% CAGR Property Segment Revenue, (2) 10% CAGR Adjusted EBITDA, and (3) 9% CAGR AFFO per share.

American Tower also has a strong balance sheet, highlighted by a recent credit upgrade by S&P from BBB- to BBB-flat. In addition, American Tower has seen strong synergies related to its CoreSite data center acquisition that is generating low double-digit growth.

AMT has returned 4.4% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return another 25% by YE 2025, which equates to around 30% annualized returns in 2025. Thus, I maintain a BUY rating as shares are now trading at 18.2x (normal is 25x) with a dividend yield of 3.4%.

Realty Income: +7.9% Since January 1st

Realty Income (O) is a net lease REIT that owns over 15,500 properties in 50 states and Europe. The highly diversified portfolio has over 1,550 customers operating in 90 different industries. Around 90% of portfolio rent is resilient to economic downturns and/or isolated from e-commerce pressures.

Realty Income’s scale provides the company with unique opportunities to pursue consolidation across a $14 Trillion investible universe (US and Europe). The company continues to expand across categories, more recently investing in data centers and casinos.

Realty Income’s historically stable cash flows are supported by high-quality real estate (98.2% occupancy in Q3-24 and rents at or below market at acquisition result in above 100% recapture ratios at expiration).

In addition, Realty Income has a stronger historical growth rate (5%) vs. most REITs (4.2%) and the company has generated positive earnings growth in 27 of 28 years.

The balance sheet is one of the best in the REIT sector, measured by net debt to annualized pro forma adjusted EBITDA of 5.4x and a fixed charge coverage ratio of 4.6x. The A-rated REIT has $5.2 billion of liquidity and only 3.4% of outstanding debt that has variable rates.

O has returned 7.9% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return another 23% by YE 2025, which equates to around 30% annualized returns in 2025. Thus, I maintain a BUY rating as shares are now trading at 13.6x (normal is 18.5x) with a dividend yield of 5.6%.

Ryman Hospitality: -4.6%Since January 1st

Ryman Hospitality (RHP) is a lodging REIT that owns 13 upscale convention center resorts and entertainment properties and a 70% controlling ownership interest in Opry Entertainment Group (OEG) and Block 21, a mixed-use entertainment, lodging, office and retail complex, including the W Austin Hotel and the ACL Live at the Moody Theater, located in downtown Austin.

I selected this lodging REIT because of its differentiated platform, in which the company focuses on large groups that offer peer-leading visibility and lower volatility. In addition, customer retention and rotation results in meaningful recurring revenue.

Group demand is growing, while competitive supply and new ground-up development remain structurally limited.

Also, Ryman has a long-tenured management team (they built the Gaylord Hotels brand and led the REIT conversion in 2013) in which operational knowledge drives more effective manager engagement.

More recently, Ryman has made a strategic investment in a leading independent music festival business known as Southern Entertainment, which creates a scalable platform for live music experiences. The partnership has one such festival in my hometown of Greenville, SC.

At the end of 2024, Ryman had $478 million of unrestricted cash on hand and the $700 million revolving credit facility was undrawn. The company’s total liquidity was approximately $1.2 billion. Also, at the end of Q4-24 the net leverage ratio was 3.9x.

As expected, Ryman posted solid 2024 results that included consolidated revenue growth of 8%, consolidated adjusted EBITDAre growth of 10% and AFFO growth of 12%. In 2025, Ryman expects to invest capital of approximately $400 million to $500 million, primarily at Gaylord Opryland and Gaylord Texan.

RHP has returned -4.55% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return 20% by YE 2025, which equates to around 15% annualized returns in 2025.

I’m maintaining a BUY, however, I’m less bullish with the lodging sector as I see a higher probability of a mild recession later this year (see my article HERE). Shares are now trading at 12.5x (normal is 16.2x) with a dividend yield of 4.6%.

Alexandria: -.68% Since January 1st

Alexandria Real Estate (ARE) is a life science REIT that owns collaborative Megacampus™ ecosystems in AAA life science innovation cluster locations, including Greater Boston, the San Francisco Bay Area, San Diego, Seattle, Maryland, Research Triangle, and New York City.

The company owns 39.8 million square feet of operating properties and 4.4 million square feet of Class A/A+ properties undergoing construction.

Alexandria has a diversified portfolio that includes 800 tenants that drive stable and long-duration cash flows. Around 92% of the Top 20 tenants ate investment-grade or publicly traded large-cap tenants. The company achieved 99.8% average tenant collection through 2024.

Alexandria has a strong balance sheet with great liquidity ($5.7 billion). At the end of Q4-24 the REIT has low leverage (5.2x) with one of the lowest debt maturities among all S&P 500 REITs (12.7 year remaining debt term and 14% of debt maturing over next 3 years).

Alexandria is in the top 10 credit ratings among all publicly traded REITs (S&P: BBB+ and Moody’s: Baa1).

The company also has a strong history of recycling assets, having completed over $9.6 billion of dispositions since 209 (73 transactions) netting $2.9 billion in proceeds (average cap rate of 5.4%).

Alexandria has guided for flat growth in 2025 and analysts’ consensus growth is 3% in 2026 and 5% in 2027. I see value in M&A as it relates to the new Trump administration, as Alexandria should benefit from upgraded tenant credit and expansion for certain pharma consolidators.

ARE has returned -.68% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return another 25% by YE 2025, which equates to around 25% annualized returns in 2025. Thus, I maintain a STRONG BUY rating as shares are now trading at 10.1x (normal is 19.8x) with a dividend yield of 5.5%.

Americold: +4.3% Since January 1st

Americold (COLD) is a REIT that owns temperature-controlled logistics real estate and value-added services.

The company owns and/or operates a global footprint that includes 239 temperature-controlled warehouses (3,200 customers), with approximately 1.5 billion refrigerated cubic feet of storage, in North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and South America.

These assets are considered “mission-critical”, temperature-controlled real estate infrastructure and tenants include companies such as Conagra, Walmart, HEB, General Mills, Ocean Spray, and many others.

COLD has a strong performance in 2024 that included a 16% increase in AFFO per share compared to 2023. In addition, same-store NOI increased by 11% year-over-year and the company guided for AFFO per share in 2025 of $1.51 to $1.59 per share (5% at the midpoint).

COLD also maintains a solid balance sheet (Fitch: BBB, Moody’s Baa3) with around $922 million in available liquidity. At the end of Q4-24, net debt to core EBITDA was approximately 5.4x and 95% unsecured debt.

COLD has returned 4.3% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return another 25% by YE 2025, which equates to around 30% annualized returns in 2025. Thus, I maintain a STRONG BUY rating as shares are now trading at 14.9x (normal is 26.7x) with a dividend yield of 6.7%.

NNN REIT: +3.6% Since January 1st

NNN REIT (NNN) is a net lease REIT thar owns 3,568 properties (385 tenants) that operate in 37 lines of trade (categories). The company focuses on smaller properties with an average cost of around $3 million and an average footprint of around 11,000 square feet.

Compared with other net lease REITs, NNN delivered modest growth of 1.8% (FFO per share) in 2024 and a lower acquisition volume of $500 million last year. However, occupancy was strong at 98.5%.

NNN has always maintained a strong balance sheet (BBB+ / Baa1) highlighted by nothing outstanding on its $1.2 billion line of credit. In addition, net debt to EBITDA at the end of Q4-24 was 5.5x and interest coverage and fixed charge coverage was 4.2x. NNN has no secured debt.

NNN initiated 2025 core FFO guidance at a range of $3.33 to $3.38 per share and 2025 AFFO guidance with a range of $3.39 to $3.44 per share (2% growth at the midpoint).

NNN has returned 3.6% since January 1st and I expect to see shares return another 20% by YE 2025, which equates to around 24% annualized returns in 2025. Thus, I maintain a BUY rating as shares are now trading at 12.3x (normal is 14.8x) with a dividend yield of 5.6%.
